name: Soutomaior Castle and Monte Monzón Loop
name_original: Castillo de Soutomayor a Monte Monzón — circular por los Puentes del río Verdugo
description: Hike a moderate 9.4-mile (15.1 km) circular route from Castillo de Soutomayor to Monte Monzón, featuring river bridges and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Soutomaior offers diverse landscapes, influenced by the Suido mountain range, the Ría de Vigo, and significant river systems. The terrain features river valleys, including the Verdugo River and its tributaries, the Oitavén and Pequeno, which flow into the San Simón inlet. Forests with native species and eucalyptus contribute to the varied natural environment. This region provides a range of hiking experiences, from riverside paths to elevated viewpoints. Best hiking trails around Soutomaior The most popular hiking route is Croa Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This easy route allows for exploration of salt flats and offers views of Lake Castiñeiras. Another top favourite among local hikers is Soutomaior Castle and Monte Monzón Loop, a moderate 9.4 miles (15.1 km) path. This route features river bridges and panoramic vistas, including views of Soutomaior Castle. Local hikers also love the El Monte Monzón from Castillo de Soutomayor — circular, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through the Monte Monzón area, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Soutomaior is defined by river valleys, mountain views, and the historical and botanical richness of Soutomaior Castle. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Soutomaior are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 900 reviews. More than 6,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Soutomaior's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Soutomaior?
A: Soutomaior offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 90 trails available. These routes cater to different fitness levels, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging ascents.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Soutomaior?
A: Hikers in Soutomaior will encounter diverse landscapes, including river valleys formed by the Verdugo, Oitavén, and Pequeno rivers, which flow into the San Simón inlet. The region also features forests with native species and eucalyptus, as well as elevated points offering views of the Ría de Vigo and the Suido mountain range.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Soutomaior has several easy trails. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375730094/croa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Croa Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.3 km route that allows you to explore salt flats and enjoy views of Lake Castiñeiras. There are 37 easy routes in total, providing plenty of choices for a gentler experience.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Soutomaior?
A: Yes, many trails in Soutomaior are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374933484/el-monte-monzon-from-castillo-de-soutomayor-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Monte Monzón from Castillo de Soutomayor — circular&lt;/a&gt; is an 11.0 km loop through the Monte Monzón area, offering a complete experience without retracing your steps.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I explore on the trails?
A: Soutomaior is rich in historical and natural attractions. You can visit the historic Soutomaior Castle and its extensive botanical park, which boasts over 400 camellia specimens. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375725347/mirador-de-caritaina-from-the-puente-de-sampaio-circular-by-the-rio-verdugo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Caritaina from the Puente de Sampaio&lt;/a&gt; route will take you past the historic Ponte Sampaio Bridge. You might also encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall on the Senda del Agua&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1620962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulló Salt Flats&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic views along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Soutomaior offers several scenic viewpoints. From elevated points like the Alto de O Viso, you can enjoy expansive views over the Ría de Vigo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374950078/soutomaior-castle-and-monte-monzon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soutomaior Castle and Monte Monzón Loop&lt;/a&gt; also features panoramic vistas. You can also find specific viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Monte A Peneda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Soutomaior?
A: The trails in Soutomaior are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 900 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to mountain views, and the historical richness of sites like Soutomaior Castle.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls along some trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall on the Senda del Agua&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural feature that can be explored while hiking in the region.

Q: Is Soutomaior part of any longer pilgrimage routes?
A: Yes, Soutomaior is part of the Portuguese Way of St. James, offering opportunities for multi-day trekking for experienced hikers who wish to undertake a pilgrimage.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Soutomaior?
A: The region's climate generally allows for hiking throughout much of the year. However, the Río Tomeza Riverside Trail, for example, can be prone to flooding during the rainy season, so it's advisable to check local conditions, especially in autumn and winter.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers?
A: Yes, the region is defined by its waterways. The Río Tomeza Riverside Trail offers a linear 4-kilometer route alongside the Tomeza River, characterized by forests and the sounds of water. The Verdugo River and its tributaries also provide picturesque settings for many trails.
tips:
This spectacular castle has its origins in a 12th century fortification. It was located here to dominate the valley of the Verdugo River and control communications from north to south. Furthermore, it was close to the coast but far enough away to be safe from raids by pirates, Normans or Turks.

In the 15th century it went from being a defense tower to a fortress with a double walled enclosure. At that time, the nobleman Pedro Álvarez de Sotomayor occupied the castle. In the 19th century, the Marquis de la Vega de Armijo had the Galería de Damas built, converting part of the fortress into a neo-Gothic palace that the family used as a summer residence. The surrounding park and gardens were also added at this time.

The next owner was the niece of the marquis, María Vinyals, a writer, polyglot and tireless defender of women. Known as the Red Marchioness, she turned the castle into a meeting place for artists.

The castle went up for auction in 1917, entering a period of decline that ended when in 1982 it was acquired by the Pontevedra Provincial Council, which completely rehabilitated it and turned its interior into a museum.

At 472 m above sea level, the Monźon peak is the highest point in the municipality of Soutomaior. Spectacular views of the Ría de Vigo to the west and the surrounding mountains to the east. Great hiking trails through the woods.

A beautiful suspension bridge over the Verdugo River, linking the wild swimming beaches on both sides of the river. The river landscape is something worth admiring. Immerse yourself in a warm summer day.
An important trade route crossing the Verdugo River, dating back to Roman times, the Puente de Comba is still used today. Its beautiful arches are reflected in the river water.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The circular route from Castillo de Soutomayor to Monte Monzón is approximately 15.1 kilometers (about 9.4 miles) long. Hikers can expect to complete it in around 4 hours and 30 minutes, depending on pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While the paths are generally accessible, it requires a good level of physical fitness and sure-footedness. It involves an elevation gain of about 480 meters and a similar descent.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, including forested areas and picturesque natural landscapes. You'll encounter the scenic Suspension Bridge of the Verdugo River and the Comboa Bridge, which are defining features. From Monte Monzón, you can enjoy spectacular panoramic views of the Ría de Vigo and surrounding mountains.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the historic Soutomaior Castle, the summit of Monte Monzón with its expansive views, and the charming bridges over the Verdugo River, such as the Suspension Bridge and Comboa Bridge.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near the castle grounds, and to be mindful of other hikers.

faqs.question: Where can I park, and is there public transport access to the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available near the Castillo de Soutomayor, which serves as a common starting point for the route. For public transport, you would need to check local bus schedules to Soutomaior, as direct access to the castle might vary.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No specific hiking permits are required to walk this trail. While the Castillo de Soutomayor itself is a museum and botanical park that may have an entrance fee if you wish to explore inside, the hiking route around it does not require a fee.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are often considered the best seasons. During these times, the weather is typically milder, and the natural scenery, especially the camellia gardens at the castle, is particularly vibrant or colorful.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can find cafes and restaurants in the vicinity of Soutomaior, particularly closer to the Castillo de Soutomayor, where you can grab a bite before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sendeiro das Greas, Sendeiro Rural de Galicia, Ponte do Canal, A Ponte Colgante, and A Ponte Vella, offering opportunities to explore further if you wish.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this moderate hike?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and length, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection, and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also advisable.

waypoints.name: Suspension Bridge of the Verdugo River
